decks.notes: This deck focuses on using Goryo's Vengeance to quickly reanimate powerful creatures like Griselbrand and Ulamog, the Defiler for explosive plays. The combination of instant reanimation spells and disruptive cards like Thoughtseize and Force of Negation help protect the combo pieces while setting up the win condition. Atraxa, Grand Unifier provides additional support and synergy with the deck's game plan. The deck also includes removal spells like Fatal Push and Consign to Memory to deal with threats on the opponent's side of the battlefield. Overall, the strategy revolves around quickly reanimating big threats and overwhelming the opponent.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on quickly and aggressively attacking the opponent using a mix of efficient creatures, burn spells, and combat tricks. Key cards like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Guide of Souls provide powerful boosts to the board, while cards like Galvanic Discharge and Goblin Bombardment help to remove threats. The deck also utilizes cards like Seasoned Pyromancer and Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer for card advantage and additional pressure. With a combination of early aggression and disruptive elements like Mana Tithe and Static Prison, this deck aims to overwhelm opponents before they have a chance to stabilize.

This deck focuses on creating a powerful combo using Broodscale Bloodchief, Glaring Fleshraker, and Blade of the Bloodchief to quickly generate powerful tokens and overwhelm the opponent. The deck also includes key support cards like Ancient Stirrings, Kozilek's Command, and Malevolent Rumble to control the board and protect the combo pieces. With Eldrazi Temple and Urza's Saga providing mana acceleration, this deck aims to quickly assemble its combo pieces and unleash a devastating offensive strategy to secure victory.

The deck focuses on ramping up mana quickly to cast powerful Eldrazi creatures, utilizing control elements to disrupt the opponent's game plan. Cards like Green Sun's Zenith and Turntimber Symbiosis help search for key pieces, while Fight Rigging and Malevolent Rumble provide removal and board control. Emrakul, the Promised End is the main win condition, supported by cards like Slumbering Trudge and Sowing Mycospawn. Disciple of Freyalise and Eldrazi Temple help accelerate mana production, allowing for big plays early in the game. Overall, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with efficient ramp and powerful threats.

The main focus of this Boros Ponza control deck is to disrupt and control the opponent's resources while slowly building up its own board presence. This is achieved through a combination of removal spells, land destruction, and protection spells. The deck aims to slow down the opponent's game plan by destroying their lands, countering key spells, and removing threats from the board. It also utilizes creatures with flash and protection abilities to maintain board presence and pressure the opponent. The inclusion of Kaheera as a companion adds an additional layer of utility and synergy to the deck.

The deck focuses on quickly developing a board presence with efficient, aggressive creatures while utilizing flicker effects to gain additional value or protect key threats. It combines disruption and removal spells to control the game, allowing the aggressive creatures to push through damage and overwhelm opponents. The deck also includes cards that can generate card advantage and provide versatility in different situations. By efficiently utilizing blink effects and aggression, the deck aims to close out games before opponents can stabilize or mount a defense.

The deck focuses on generating mana efficiently through cards like Desperate Ritual, Manamorphose, and Ruby Medallion to quickly cast spells and ultimately combo off with cards like Ral, Monsoon Mage and Glimpse of the Impossible. The goal is to chain together multiple spells to create a storm count and finish off the opponent with a massive Grapeshot. Past in Flames allows for graveyard recursion and reusing key spells, while Wish provides flexibility in finding important pieces from the sideboard. The deck aims to quickly overwhelm the opponent with a flurry of spells and burn damage.

This Modern UW Control deck focuses on using counterspells and removal spells to control the board and protect its planeswalkers. The deck includes cards like Counterspell, Teferi, Time Raveler, and Supreme Verdict to control the game and maintain board presence. The deck also includes cards like Isochron Scepter and Prismatic Ending for additional flexibility and removal options. The companion Kaheera, the Orphanguard provides additional value and synergy with the deck's creature cards. Overall, the key concept of this deck is to maintain control of the game and outlast the opponent's threats.

The deck focuses on utilizing the Cascade mechanic with cards like Bloodbraid Marauder to cascade into Living End, allowing the player to fill their graveyard with creatures and then bring them all back onto the battlefield. The deck also utilizes Leyline of the Guildpact to help protect their creatures once they are on the battlefield. The deck includes a mix of powerful creatures like Scion of Draco and Oliphaunt to help overwhelm the opponent once Living End is resolved. Overall, the deck aims to quickly assemble its combo pieces and overwhelm the opponent with a massive board presence.

The main idea of this Boros Aggro deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and aggressive strategy. The deck focuses on utilizing powerful creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls to deal damage quickly. Cards like Goblin Bombardment and Galvanic Discharge help to control the board and clear the way for your creatures to attack. The inclusion of Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Voice of Victory provide additional support and damage potential. Overall, the deck aims to apply early pressure and maintain a strong board presence to secure a swift victory.

This Dimir Control deck focuses on disruption, removal, and card advantage to dominate the game. It utilizes powerful spells like Thoughtseize and Counterspell to control the board, along with creatures like Tasigur and Quantum Riddler to provide additional threats. The deck also includes various removal spells such as Fatal Push and Force of Despair to deal with opposing creatures. The card selection is supplemented by powerful planeswalkers like Tamiyo and unique creatures like Psychic Frog. Overall, the deck aims to outvalue and outmaneuver the opponent through careful resource management and strategic play.

The deck focuses on using instant reanimation spells like Goryo's Vengeance to cheat out powerful creatures like Griselbrand and Solitude. It also includes cards like Ephemerate and Flood of Tears to protect and recur creatures. The deck aims to quickly load the graveyard with creatures using cards like Faithful Mending and Psychic Frog, then reanimate them for a powerful combo finish. The deck's instant speed plays allow for flexibility and surprise plays to outmaneuver opponents in a 1 vs 1 format.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly play aggressive creatures and disrupt the opponent's game plan through hand disruption, removal spells, and artifact hate. The synergy between cards like Mishra's Bauble and Nethergoyf help to generate card advantage and power up the creatures on the battlefield. The Eldrazi creatures also provide a strong presence on the board, putting pressure on the opponent while Thought-Knot Seer disrupts their hand further. The land base supports the strategy by providing color fixing and additional utility.

This deck focuses on fast aggression and consistent damage output through a combination of low cost creatures such as Monastery Swiftspear and Cori-Steel Cutter, along with burn spells like Lightning Bolt and Lava Dart. The deck also utilizes card selection and manipulation with cards like Expressive Iteration and Preordain to maintain a high tempo. Abilities like Mutagenic Growth and Violent Urge are used to pump up creatures for additional damage. The mana base is designed for efficiency and consistency, allowing for quick access to the necessary colors for spells and creatures.

Utilizing powerful Eldrazi creatures and the UrzaTron lands to ramp up quickly, this Control deck aims to control the game by disrupting the opponent's strategy and answering threats with removal spells and versatile artifacts. The deck's goal is to stall the game until it can assemble the UrzaTron lands and cast huge threats like Ugin, the Spirit Dragon to dominate the battlefield. With a mix of disruption, ramp, and high-impact threats, this deck looks to overwhelm opponents with its late-game power.

The main concept of this deck is to control the game by utilizing the powerful UrzaTron lands to ramp up mana quickly and cast high-impact Eldrazi creatures and planeswalkers. The deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's game plan with cards like Trinisphere and Thought-Knot Seer while also being able to remove threats with Dismember and Ugin, the Eye of the Storms. By combining control elements with big threats like Emrakul and Ulamog, the Ceaseless Hunger, the deck aims to dominate the late game and secure victory.
